description: AI-assisted curated matches for cave register records whose bibliographic name is inflected, abbreviated, translated or context-dependent. The target cave_number must exist in the official SMOPaJ cave register import.

sources.title: Codex GPT-5.5-style review of unmatched cave register cards
sources.note: Only high-confidence links were accepted. Ambiguous cards were deferred for manual or deeper contextual review.

matches.note: Bibliographic group name for the Demänovské jaskyne cave system; canonical official parent record is Demänovský jaskynný systém.
Short form used in article titles and abstracts for Demänovská jaskyňa slobody.
English Aragonit records explicitly refer to Dobšinská ľadová jaskyňa.
Genitive bibliographic form of Ardovská jaskyňa.
Inflected bibliographic form of Drienovská jaskyňa.
Genitive bibliographic form of Drienovská jaskyňa.
Inflected title form of Ladzianskeho jaskyňa.
Genitive bibliographic form of Ladzianskeho jaskyňa.
Genitive bibliographic form of Pružinská Dúpna jaskyňa.
Locative bibliographic form of Pružinská Dúpna jaskyňa.
Reversed title form of Pružinská Dúpna jaskyňa.
Czech bibliographic title form of Pružinská Dúpna jaskyňa.
Article context places the cave in Vrátna dolina / Malá Fatra, matching official Medvedia jaskyňa II.
Article context repeatedly places this Medvedia jaskyňa in Stratenská hornatina / Slovenský raj; official record 4692 is k. ú. Letanovce, Slovenský raj.
Article context places this Medvedia jaskyňa in Jánska dolina; official record 1810 is k. ú. Liptovský Ján and alias Zimná jaskyňa.
Article context points to Malužinský kras and Bocianska dolina in Nízke Tatry, not the Muránska planina cave with the same name.
Article context points to Malé Karpaty, selecting the Malé Karpaty Majkova jaskyňa rather than the Slovenský kras record.
Article context mentions Krakova hoľa / Nízke Tatry, selecting the Nízke Tatry Javorová priepasť.
Article context mentions Pustá - Psie diery and the Demänovský cave system, selecting Pustá jaskyňa in Nízke Tatry.
Article context mentions Jánska dolina / Nízke Tatry, selecting official Sokolová in Nízke Tatry.
Adjectival bibliographic form of Sokolová in Nízke Tatry.
Article context mentions Malý Rozsutec, selecting official Kryštálová jaskyňa in Malá Fatra / Rozsutce.
User confirmed Domica == Baradla in bibliographic context; Baradla is the Hungarian-side/name context, while the Slovak SMOPaJ cave record is Domica.
User confirmed Baradla should be treated as Domica in this bibliography.
English bibliographic form of Baradla; user confirmed it belongs under Domica.
Bibliographic form Jaskyňa Baradla is grouped under Domica per user confirmation.
Bibliographic Domica-Baradla system records are grouped under the Slovak SMOPaJ cave Domica for the cave timeline.
User confirmed Kľúčová dierka is the locality/context of research or discovery in Domica, not the separate SMOPaJ cave Kľúčová dierka.
User confirmed Javorinka equals bibliographic Jaskyňa Javorinka; article context places it in Vysoké Tatry / Javorová dolina, matching official child record 5930.1, not the Ždiar cave 5842.

article_matches.note: Článok lokalizuje Jaskyňu Oltárkameň v Čergove; SMOPaJ 211 eviduje Zbojnícku dieru s aliasom Oltárkameň.
Fulltext Slovenský kras 1985 uvádza kataster obce Šambron a severný okraj Levočských vrchov.
Článok Aragonit 2010 opakovane uvádza Zbojnícku dieru pri Švošove.
Článok Slovenský kras 2014 uvádza Jaskyňu Zbojnícka diera pri Švošove vo Veľkej Fatre.
Článok Aragonit 2018 uvádza Zbojnícku džuru pri Sulíne; SMOPaJ 2401 má alias Zbojnícka džura.

deferred.reason: Several official Okno records exist. Article context suggests Demänovská dolina, but the exact official record still needs verification.
Likely linked to Štefanová 1 in Demänovská dolina, but the bibliographic card may represent a broader local name.
The current card appears to mix Dračia jaskyňa with the Dračia jaskyňa alias of Demänovská ľadová jaskyňa.
The card mixes Branisko and Javorníky contexts and also possible ponor/vyvieračka records.
The card mixes at least Dubná skala / Malá Fatra and Dobšinsko-stratenský system contexts.
